title: Callicarpa peichieniana
taxonomy: Callicarpa peichieniana is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a type of plant that belongs to the genus Callicarpa, which is a group of plants known for their vibrant purple berries. The plant is native to Taiwan and is often grown for its ornamental value. Callicarpa peichieniana is a shrub that typically grows up to 1-2 meters in height and has small, fragrant flowers. The plant's classification is further supported by its placement in the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics.

intro: Callicarpa peichieniana is a species of plant in the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the purple fruit verbena or purple bellflower. The plant has been traditionally used in Taiwanese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections, due to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties.
